scenario_config.name: national_medium
_dataset_notes: 10 teachers, 4 classrooms each, 25 students = 1,000 students total.
Each teacher has 100 students — representative of a teacher with 4 typical class sections.
8 weeks history: 6 cold (compacted to rollup tables), 2 hot (raw rows in Run/TurnEvent).
Hot data: 1,000 students x 2 hot weeks x 3 runs x 6 turns = ~36,000 hot turn events.
Rollup tables hold 6 weeks of compacted history per student.
Analytics queries test at realistic per-teacher scale and with meaningful cold history depth.
Dataset prep will take several minutes due to bulk inserts and compaction of ~108,000 cold turns.
WARNING: This scenario will produce load_health: red with a 3-worker Gunicorn setup.
That is expected and intentional — the purpose is to measure the gap, not to pass.
_traffic_notes: Derived from the national load model: medium adoption (50% of 60,000 grade 3-5 pupils = 30,000 students).
Peak concurrent students at medium adoption: ~1,580.
Ingest RPS: 1,580 students x 1.3 turns/min / 60 = ~35 ingest/sec.
Teacher traffic: 1,580 students / 20 per class = ~80 concurrent teachers.
Dashboard RPS: 80 teachers x 1 reload/min / 60 = ~1.3 RPS → rounded to 2 RPS.
Analytics and turn_insights: teachers poll section data; 1 RPS each is representative.
VUs are sized generously so that drops come from server response time, not VU starvation.
At ingest latency of 500ms under load: 35 x 0.5 = 17.5 steady-state VUs; pre=80 gives 4x headroom.
Expected outcome with current infra (3 Gunicorn workers): load_health red, high drop rate on ingest.
Use the drop rate and latency at completion to estimate required worker count.
